select_when: Attention placement will shape how the next message is interpreted almost as much as the message itself.
The audience is overloaded, distracted, or relying on default associations, so receptivity must be created before analysis begins.
A complex idea needs a simple, salient entry frame before it can get a fair hearing.
Sequencing and salience determine whether the audience will even consider the argument on its merits.
avoid_when: Receptivity engineering is crossing into manipulation theater decoupled from the actual merits of the proposal.
The audience is already in deep analytical mode, so associative priming would add less value than direct evidence and clear logic.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model on preliminary attention guidance and frame-setting rather than broad persuasion or copywriting sprawl.
donor_drops: Did not widen into general storytelling or sales technique catalogs because the raw source is strongest on sequencing, salience, and associative preparation.
open_questions: The output label compresses a source that spans attention cues, emotional sale, and physical-context priming.

select_when.note: The strengths and playbook sections explicitly support attention placement, overloaded audiences, and making complex ideas legible before defense.

avoid_when.note: Both avoid conditions come directly from the raw warnings about manipulation and the limits of the model under deliberative scrutiny.

input_type.note: The source activates where attention shape matters before the ask, while the compact type label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The playbook yields a pre-message framing state that makes the later argument legible; the output label compresses that surface.

reasoning_types.note: The model reasons causally about associative triggers and metacognitively about how salience shapes later interpretation.
